metadata.scheme_name: Reimbursement of Fees of ST Students Studying in Sainik School Private Institutions

raw_data.details: The scheme "Reimbursement of Fees for ST Students Studying in Sainik Schools and Private Institutions," implemented by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides free arrangements for accommodation, school fees, and education for eligible students.
raw_data.benefits: Free arrangement for accommodation, school fees and studies for the students.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant must be a native of Madhya Pradesh. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) category. The applicant must be enrolled in a recognized Sainik School or private institution covered under the scheme.
raw_data.application_process:
Offline
Step 1: The interested applicant should connect to the Office of the District Magistrate and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the concerned authority.
Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the authority.
Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable). NOTE: Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period.
raw_data.documents_required: Passport-size PhotographAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste Certificate Previous Class Passing Certificate / MarksheetSamagra IDBank Account DetailsProof of Enrolment into a Sainik School

To know more please visit Check EligibilityReimbursement of Fees of ST Students Studying in Sainik School Private InstitutionsMadhya PradeshReimbursement of Fees of ST Students Studying in Sainik School Private InstitutionsReimbursementSainik SchoolScheduled TribeStudentsCheck EligibilityDetailsThe scheme "Reimbursement of Fees for ST Students Studying in Sainik Schools and Private Institutions," implemented by the Tribal Welfare and Scheduled Caste (SC) Department, Government of Madhya Pradesh, provides free arrangements for accommodation, school fees, and education for eligible students. BenefitsFree arrangement for accommodation, school fees and studies for the students. EligibilityThe applicant must be a native of Madhya Pradesh. The applicant should belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) category. The applicant must be enrolled in a recognized Sainik School or private institution covered under the scheme. Application ProcessOfflineStep 1: The interested applicant should connect to the Office of the District Magistrate and request the hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the concerned authority. Step 2: In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, paste the passport-sized photograph (signed across, if required), and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required). Step 3: Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents, within the prescribed period (if any), to the authority. Step 4: Request a receipt or acknowledgement from the concerned authority to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable). NOTE: Ensure that the application is submitted within the prescribed period. Documents RequiredPassport-size PhotographAadhaar CardDomicile CertificateCaste Certificate Previous Class Passing Certificate / MarksheetSamagra IDBank Account DetailsProof of Enrolment into a Sainik SchoolFrequently Asked QuestionsWhat is this scheme about? The scheme provides free arrangements for accommodation, school fees, and studies for Scheduled Tribe (ST) students studying in Sainik Schools and private institutions. Who is eligible for this scheme? The applicant must be a native of Madhya Pradesh and belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) category.

What types of expenses are covered under the scheme? The scheme covers school fees, accommodation, and other related study expenses. Does the scheme apply to students in private institutions? Yes, the scheme is applicable to students studying in recognized private institutions and Sainik Schools. Is there an income limit for eligibility? No, there is no specific income limit mentioned for eligibility under this scheme. What documents are required to apply? Applicants need to provide domicile proof, caste certificate, admission proof, and fee receipts. How can I apply for the scheme? Applications can be submitted through the concerned Tribal Welfare Office or via the online application portal, if available. Is the scheme applicable to all classes? Yes, it is applicable to students of all grades studying in recognized Sainik Schools or private institutions. Can students outside Madhya Pradesh apply for this scheme? No, the scheme is exclusively for natives of Madhya Pradesh. Sources And References[Scheme Details](https://cmhelpline.mp.gov.in/KnowYourEntitleDetail.aspx?status=ByVibhag&pointvalue=2&Schemeid=893)OkWas this helpful?
raw_data.scheme_name: Reimbursement of Fees of ST Students Studying in Sainik School Private Institutions

faqs.question: What is this scheme about?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides free arrangements for accommodation, school fees, and studies for Scheduled Tribe (ST) students studying in Sainik Schools and private institutions.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The applicant must be a native of Madhya Pradesh and belong to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) category.

faqs.question: What types of expenses are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers school fees, accommodation, and other related study expenses.

faqs.question: Does the scheme apply to students in private institutions?
faqs.answer: Yes, the scheme is applicable to students studying in recognized private institutions and Sainik Schools.

faqs.question: Is there an income limit for eligibility?
faqs.answer: No, there is no specific income limit mentioned for eligibility under this scheme.

faqs.question: What documents are required to apply?
faqs.answer: Applicants need to provide domicile proof, caste certificate, admission proof, and fee receipts.

faqs.question: How can I apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: Applications can be submitted through the concerned Tribal Welfare Office or via the online application portal, if available.

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable to all classes?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is applicable to students of all grades studying in recognized Sainik Schools or private institutions.

faqs.question: Can students outside Madhya Pradesh apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: No, the scheme is exclusively for natives of Madhya Pradesh.
